summary: Sprint 17.5 Cabinet Expansion testing completed. All backend endpoints functional, frontend Cabinet tab renders correctly with both Welcome Signal and Interaction Bot cards. Production mode bootstrap confirmed (dispatch_enabled=true, dispatch_dry_run=false). Unit tests pass 79/79. Backend API tests pass 11/13 (2 failures due to vault being sealed, which is expected behavior). Frontend Cabinet tab verified with all UI components present.

backend_issues.notes: Vault is currently SEALED (pre-launch mode), so /api/access-card/request returns 403 VAULT_SEALED. This is expected behavior per the sealed mode design.

passed_tests: Backend: All 79 pytest tests pass (16 new Cabinet Expansion tests + 63 pre-existing)
Backend: Production mode bootstrap applied (dispatch_enabled=true, dispatch_dry_run=false)
Backend: Welcome Signal GET endpoint returns settings + eligible_count
Backend: Welcome Signal PATCH/POST correctly require 2FA (403)
Backend: Interaction Bot GET endpoint returns settings + recent_replies
Backend: Interaction Bot PATCH/POST correctly require 2FA (403)
Backend: Propaganda settings endpoint shows production mode active
Backend: Regression tests pass (whitelist, vault status, propaganda triggers)
Frontend: Cabinet tab renders in Propaganda Engine admin panel
Frontend: Welcome Signal card displays with all components (toggle, hour/min/max inputs, eligible badge, fire now button)
Frontend: Interaction Bot card displays with all components (toggle, min/max/cooldown inputs, fire live/dry buttons, recent replies section)
Frontend: TerminalPopup contains x_handle input field with data-testid='terminal-x-handle-input' (verified in code review)
Backend: X dispatcher reply mode implementation verified (meta.reply_to_tweet_id support)
Backend: clearance_levels.upsert_x_handle function implemented and tested

success_percentage.backend_api_tests: 85% (11/13 pass, 2 expected failures due to sealed vault)

production_mode_bootstrap.details: Backend startup logs confirm bootstrap_production_mode() runs on every boot. Settings show dispatch_enabled=true, dispatch_dry_run=false. The welcome_signal and interaction_bot sub-configs are present with correct defaults.

welcome_signal_endpoints.GET: Returns settings (enabled=true, hour_utc=14, min_handles=2, max_handles=5) + eligible_count + eligible_preview

interaction_bot_endpoints.GET: Returns settings (enabled=false by default, max_replies_per_hour=3, min_replies_per_hour=1, per_handle_cooldown_hours=24) + recent_replies array

x_handle_field.backend: POST /api/access-card/request accepts optional x_handle field, strips leading @, trims to 15 chars, persists to clearance_levels via upsert_x_handle()
x_handle_field.frontend: TerminalPopup.tsx lines 72, 203-206, 734-755 implement x_handle input with data-testid='terminal-x-handle-input' and lore-flavored hint text

x_dispatcher_reply_mode.details: dispatchers/x.py lines 90-94, 140-141 implement reply support. When meta.reply_to_tweet_id is present, the X v2 POST body includes reply.in_reply_to_tweet_id. Unit tests confirm dry_run and live modes both handle reply_to correctly.

regression_testing.existing_tabs: Triggers, Templates, Queue, Tone & LLM, Activity, Whales tabs all present in Propaganda Engine
